name: Jan Hagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly grease a 15½ x 10½ inch jelly roll pan.
3. In a bowl, mix flour, cinnamon, and ginger on a sheet of wax paper; set aside.
4. In a large bowl, beat sugar with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
5. Beat in egg yolk until mixture is well combined.
6. Add vanilla extract to the mixture.
7. Gradually add the flour mixture and stir until completely incorporated.
8. Turn dough onto the prepared pan and pat it into an even sheet.
9. In a cup or small bowl, beat egg white and water with a sharp knife until frothy.
10. Brush the batter over the dough.
11. Sprinkle nuts evenly over the dough and press them in lightly.
12. Using a long ruler and a sharp knife, cut the dough into thirds lengthwise.
13. Cut each third crosswise into strips about 1⅓ inches wide.
14. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until the sheet is light golden brown. (If using a CushionAire cookie sheet, baking time may be slightly longer).
15. Place pan on a rack and cut along the original cutting marks.
16. Transfer strips to racks and allow them to cool completely.
17. Store in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks or up to 2 months in the freezer.
